source_paper_title: Indefinite Linear-Quadratic Partially Observed Mean-Field Game
context:
Section 4.3, Remark 4.13(ii), page 14

Noticing that Riccati equation (35) is an asymmetric indefinite equation with complex structure, its solvability is still an problem.
title: Solvability of the asymmetric indefinite Riccati equation for partially observed mean-field games
latex:
Given a time horizon \(T>0\). Let \(n,m\in\mathbb{N}\). Let \(A,\bar A,D,\bar D\in L^{\infty}([0,T];\mathbb{R}^{n\times n})\), \(B\in L^{\infty}([0,T];\mathbb{R}^{n\times m})\), \(F,\bar F\in L^{\infty}([0,T];\mathbb{R}^{n\times m})\), \(Q\in L^{\infty}([0,T];\mathbb{S}^n)\), \(R\in L^{\infty}([0,T];\mathbb{S}^m)\), \(S\in L^{\infty}([0,T];\mathbb{R}^{n\times m})\), and let \(L_T\in\mathbb{S}^n\) and constants \(\alpha_1,\alpha_2,\alpha_3,\beta_1,\beta_2\in\mathbb{R}\) be given. For an unknown matrix-valued function \(\Sigma:[0,T]\to\mathbb{R}^{n\times n}\), define for each \(t\in[0,T]\)
\[
R^e_t = (1-\beta_1)R_t + F_t^{\top}\Sigma_t(F_t+\bar F_t),
\]
\[
\Sigma^e_t = \Sigma_t(B_t+\bar B_t) + D_t^{\top}\Sigma_t(F_t+\bar F_t) + (1-\beta_2)S_t,
\]
\[
\bar\Sigma_t = \Sigma_t\bar B_t + (D_t+\bar D_t)^{\top}\Sigma_t F_t + (1-\alpha_2)S_t.
\]
Consider the terminal-value matrix Riccati differential equation
\[
\dot\Sigma_t + \Sigma_t(A_t+\bar A_t) + A_t^{\top}\Sigma_t + D_t^{\top}\Sigma_t(D_t+\bar D_t)
- \Sigma^e_t (R^e_t)^{-1} \bar\Sigma_t^{\top} + (1-\alpha_1)Q_t = 0,\qquad t\in[0,T],
\]
with terminal condition
\[
\Sigma_T = (1-\alpha_3)L_T.
\]
The problem is to give verifiable necessary and/or sufficient conditions on the coefficient data \((A,\bar A,D,\bar D,B,F,\bar F,Q,R,S,L_T,\alpha_1,\alpha_2,\alpha_3,\beta_1,\beta_2)\) under which there exists a (preferably unique) solution \(\Sigma\in C([0,T];\mathbb{R}^{n\times n})\) such that \(R^e_t\) is invertible for all \(t\in[0,T]\) (e.g., \(R^e_t+(R^e_t)^{\top}\gg 0\) for all \(t\)), and to characterize the solution when it exists.
open_problem_quote: Noticing that Riccati equation (35) is an asymmetric indefinite equation with complex structure, its solvability is still an problem.

verification.notice: This entry was generated from the paper text only. It has not been independently verified as still open. If you believe the question is solved or incorrectly stated, please contact the maintainers.
